expectations.text: Report includes separate grades for typography, color, spacing, layout, and polish
expectations.evidence: Report has a grading table with separate grades for all 5 dimensions: Typography (A), Color (A), Spacing (A), Layout (A), Polish (B+)

expectations.text: Overall grade is A or B (the described design follows best practices)
expectations.evidence: Overall grade is explicitly 'A' at the top of the report

expectations.text: Report includes anti-slop checklist
expectations.evidence: Report includes a full 'Anti-Slop Checklist' section with 9 checkboxes covering: no purple-to-pink gradients, no generic Inter, max 2 fonts, no text < 14px, no wide centered text, spacing on 8px grid, contrast passes AA, no pure white/black, icon consistency

expectations.text: Report includes separate grades for typography, color, spacing, layout, and polish
expectations.evidence: Report has numbered sections for Typography (1), Color System (2), Spacing & Layout (3), Card Component (4), Accessibility (5). It covers typography, color, spacing, and layout but does NOT have a 'polish' dimension or letter grades per section. Sections have verdicts like 'Strong', 'Good pairing', 'Correct approach' but not formal letter grades for each dimension.

expectations.text: Overall grade is A or B (the described design follows best practices)
expectations.evidence: Overall grade is 'B+' stated at the top of the report, which falls within the A or B range

expectations.text: Report includes anti-slop checklist
expectations.evidence: Report does not include an anti-slop checklist. It mentions 'purple-to-pink' or gradient concerns nowhere, and has no checklist-style section evaluating common anti-patterns.

expectations.text: Report includes separate grades for typography, color, spacing, layout, and polish
expectations.evidence: Report includes separate graded sections: Color System (B+), Typography (A-), Spacing & Layout (A), Components (B), Dark Mode (A-), Accessibility (B). Covers all requested dimensions with individual grades.

expectations.text: Overall grade is A or B (the described design follows best practices)
expectations.evidence: Overall grade is B+, which falls within the A/B range. Report notes 'Solid foundation with professional choices across typography, color, and spacing.'

expectations.text: Report includes anti-slop checklist
expectations.evidence: Section '7. Anti-Pattern Check' contains a table checking: AI purple gradients (TRIGGERED), more than 2 accent colors (PASS), no loading states, thin fonts for data (PASS), color-only information, placeholder-as-label, generated SVG icons, inconsistent icon stroke weight

expectations.text: Report includes separate grades for typography, color, spacing, layout, and polish
expectations.evidence: Report includes separate graded sections: Typography (D+), Color (C), Layout & Spacing (B+), Visual Details & Atmosphere (D), Accessibility (A-), Differentiation (F). Each has an individual letter grade.

expectations.text: Overall grade is A or B (the described design follows best practices)
expectations.evidence: Overall grade is C+. Report states 'It will not break anything, but it will not be remembered either. The design reads as AI-generated dark SaaS template.' The described design objectively follows best practices (8px grid, AA contrast, good fonts), so a C+ is too harsh.

expectations.text: Report includes anti-slop checklist
expectations.evidence: The 'Anti-Slop Detection Summary' table at end of report checks: Generic purple/blue gradient hero (FAIL), Typography > 2 families (PASS), Color palette with no semantic meaning (FAIL), Placeholder content (UNKNOWN), Cards with inconsistent border radius (N/A), Buttons with no hover/focus (UNKNOWN), Stock illustration (FAIL)

expectations.text: Flags the 3-font violation (Inter + Playfair Display + Fira Code)
expectations.evidence: Typography grade F states: '3 fonts used (Inter, Playfair Display, Fira Code for body)' and 'Three fonts from three entirely different families (geometric sans, transitional serif, monospace) creates visual chaos'

expectations.text: Flags inconsistent border-radius (8px/16px/24px mixed)
expectations.evidence: Polish grade D states: 'Three different border-radius values (8px, 16px, 24px) on the same page with no systematic reason' and 'Mixed radii at 8/16/24px is the exact C-grade example'

expectations.text: Flags 13px body text as below 14px minimum
expectations.evidence: Typography grade states: 'Body text at 13px is below the 14px minimum' and anti-slop checklist marks FAIL for 'No text < 14px — Body text is 13px, below the 14px floor'

expectations.text: Flags purple-to-pink gradient as anti-pattern
expectations.evidence: Color grade C states: 'The purple-to-pink gradient hero is the single most flagged anti-pattern in the design system.' Anti-slop checklist: 'FAIL — Purple-to-pink gradient is present on the hero. This is the #1 AI made this signal.'

expectations.text: Flags 45px or 11px spacing as not on 8px grid
expectations.evidence: Spacing grade D states: '45px section spacing is not on the 8px grid (not divisible by 8)' and '11px button padding is also off-grid (nearest valid values: 8px or 16px)'. Anti-slop checklist: 'FAIL — 45px section spacing and 11px button padding are both off-grid.'

expectations.text: Overall grade is D or F
expectations.evidence: Overall grade is explicitly 'D' at the top of the report

expectations.text: Provides specific fix recommendations
expectations.evidence: Fixes Required section provides 4 specific fix categories with actionable recommendations: '1. Reduce to 2 fonts, increase body size' (set body to 16px min), '2. Kill the gradient, fix white' (replace #FFFFFF with #E8E8E8), '3. Snap to 8px grid' (replace 45px with 96px, 11px with 16px), '4. Unify border-radius' (pick 12px or 16px)

expectations.text: Flags the 3-font violation (Inter + Playfair Display + Fira Code)
expectations.evidence: Issue 1 (High Severity): 'The design uses three different fonts — Inter, Playfair Display, and Fira Code — with Fira Code (a monospace font) assigned to body text.' Identifies all 3 fonts by name and explains why 3 is too many.

expectations.text: Flags inconsistent border-radius (8px/16px/24px mixed)
expectations.evidence: Issue 3 (Medium Severity): 'Cards use mixed border-radius values — 8px on some, 16px on others, and 24px on the CTA.' States 'A 3x range (8px to 24px) is too wide to read as intentional variation — it reads as accidental.'

expectations.text: Flags 13px body text as below 14px minimum
expectations.evidence: Issue 2 (High Severity): 'Body text is set at 13px.' States 'The widely accepted minimum for body text on the web is 16px' and '13px falls below the widely accepted floor of 16px'. Note: flags as below 16px, not 14px specifically, but does clearly flag 13px as too small.

expectations.text: Flags purple-to-pink gradient as anti-pattern
expectations.evidence: Issue 6 (Low Severity) discusses the gradient but says 'A purple-to-pink gradient is a common pattern and not inherently problematic' and only flags potential brand conflict and visual transition issues. Does NOT identify it as an anti-pattern — rates it Low Severity and treats it as acceptable with caveats.

expectations.text: Flags 45px or 11px spacing as not on 8px grid
expectations.evidence: Issue 4 (Medium Severity): 'These values do not align with any standard spacing scale (4px, 8px, multiples of 4, or multiples of 8). A 45px gap and 11px padding are odd numbers that break grid alignment.'

expectations.text: Overall grade is D or F
expectations.evidence: No overall letter grade is given. The report has a 'Summary Table' with severity ratings per issue and an 'Overall Assessment' paragraph, but no D or F grade. The assessment says 'strong foundations... but suffers from inconsistency' which implies a middling grade, not D or F.

expectations.text: Provides specific fix recommendations
expectations.evidence: Each of the 6 issues includes a 'Recommendation' section with specific fixes: 'Use Inter for body text... Remove Fira Code', 'Increase body text to 16px minimum', 'Standardize cards to a single border-radius (8px)', 'Use 48px or 64px for section spacing', 'Soften the white to #E8E8E8 or #F0F0F0'

expectations.text: Flags the 3-font violation (Inter + Playfair Display + Fira Code)
expectations.evidence: Issue 1 titled 'Three Font Families — Typographic Chaos' explicitly identifies Inter, Playfair Display, and Fira Code as the three problematic fonts, calling it Critical severity and noting the anti-pattern 'Typography using more than 2 font families'

expectations.text: Flags inconsistent border-radius (8px/16px/24px mixed)
expectations.evidence: Issue 2 titled 'Inconsistent Border-Radius Across Components' identifies 'Three different border-radius values: Cards: some at 8px, others at 16px; CTA button: 24px' as High severity

expectations.text: Flags 13px body text as below 14px minimum
expectations.evidence: Issue 4 titled '13px Body Text — Below Minimum Readable Size' states '13px body text falls between defined scale stops and sits below the 14px minimum for secondary body text', rated Critical severity

expectations.text: Flags purple-to-pink gradient as anti-pattern
expectations.evidence: Issue 3 titled 'Purple-to-Pink Gradient Hero Background' calls it High severity and states 'Purple-to-pink gradients are the single most overused aesthetic in post-2023 web design'

expectations.text: Flags 45px or 11px spacing as not on 8px grid
expectations.evidence: Issue 5 flags '45px section spacing — Off the 8px Grid' as High severity, and Issue 6 flags '11px button padding — Off-Grid and Undersized' as Medium severity

expectations.text: Overall grade is D or F
expectations.evidence: No explicit overall letter grade is given. The report uses severity classifications (Critical/High/Medium) and states 'Overall: 1 pass, 1 warning, 6 failures' but never assigns a D or F letter grade

expectations.text: Provides specific fix recommendations
expectations.evidence: Each of the 7 issues includes a 'Fix:' section with specific recommendations, e.g., 'Set body text to 16px minimum', 'Use 48px for major section breaks', 'Set button padding to 12px vertical, 16px horizontal'

expectations.text: Flags the 3-font violation (Inter + Playfair Display + Fira Code)
expectations.evidence: Issue 1 titled 'Three competing typefaces with no hierarchy logic' identifies Inter, Playfair Display, and Fira Code, calling it Critical severity and noting 'Using three fonts is not inherently wrong, but these three have no shared DNA'

expectations.text: Flags inconsistent border-radius (8px/16px/24px mixed)
expectations.evidence: Issue 3 titled 'Inconsistent border-radius across cards and CTA' identifies 'Three different border-radius values (8px, 16px, 24px)' as High severity

expectations.text: Flags 13px body text as below 14px minimum
expectations.evidence: Issue 2 titled '13px body text is below the accessibility floor' states '16px (1rem) is the universally accepted baseline for body copy on the web' and rates it Critical severity

expectations.text: Flags purple-to-pink gradient as anti-pattern
expectations.evidence: Issue 4 titled 'Purple-to-pink gradient hero is a design cliche' states 'the single most overused background treatment in AI-generated and template-driven landing pages', rated High severity

expectations.text: Flags 45px or 11px spacing as not on 8px grid
expectations.evidence: Issue 5 titled '45px section spacing breaks the 8-point grid' and Issue 6 titled '11px button padding is too thin and off-grid' both flag these values as not on the 8-point scale

expectations.text: Overall grade is D or F
expectations.evidence: Overall grade is explicitly stated as 'D+' — 'Needs a systemic rework, not incremental patches.'

expectations.text: Provides specific fix recommendations
expectations.evidence: Each issue includes detailed fix recommendations with specific values, e.g., 'Set body text to 16px minimum (1rem)', 'Use 48px for section gaps', 'button padding to 12px/16px', and a complete CSS design tokens block

expectations.text: Identifies purple-to-pink gradient as anti-pattern
expectations.evidence: Color grade F: 'Purple-to-pink gradient (#8B5CF6 to #EC4899) is the single most recognized AI made this signal in web design. It is explicitly banned in the anti-slop checklist'. Anti-slop check #1 FAIL: '#8B5CF6 to #EC4899 is exactly this pattern'

expectations.text: Flags Inter at 400 weight on muted palette as generic/timid
expectations.evidence: Typography grade F: 'Inter at 400 weight on a muted gray palette is the definition of generic AI output -- Inter requires bold brand colors to avoid looking templated.' Anti-slop check #2 FAIL: 'Inter 400 on muted gray -- textbook generic'

expectations.text: Flags centered text spanning full 1440px as too wide for center alignment
expectations.evidence: Spacing grade D: 'Centered text spanning the full 1440px container violates the max center-aligned width rule (600px max). At 12px font size, a 1440px container produces lines of ~240+ characters -- roughly 4x the recommended 65-75 character max line width.' Anti-slop check #5 FAIL.

expectations.text: Flags stock photo of people in office
expectations.evidence: Anti-slop check #6 FAIL: 'Generic people in an office stock photo'. Layout grade D: 'stock photo of people in an office answers none of them'. Fix #4: 'Stock photos of people in an office communicate nothing about your product' with 4 ranked alternatives.

expectations.text: Flags 12px body text as below 14px minimum
expectations.evidence: Typography grade F: '12px body text is below the 14px minimum for any content that isn't a badge or tag'. Anti-slop check #4 FAIL: '12px body text violates the minimum'. Fix table shows: Body size current 12px, fix to 16-18px.

expectations.text: Suggests specific alternative choices (different gradient, font, alignment)
expectations.evidence: Fix #1 offers 3 gradient alternatives (solid dark, subtle brand-tinted, no gradient). Fix #2 suggests specific display fonts: Space Grotesk, Cabinet Grotesk, Clash Display. Fix #3: 'Max content width for centered text: 600px' or 'left-align in 600-700px column'. Each fix has concrete values.

expectations.text: Identifies purple-to-pink gradient as anti-pattern
expectations.evidence: Issue #4 discusses the gradient as a 'Moderate Issue' and says it 'introduces several risks' around brand perception, contrast inconsistency, and saturation fatigue. But it does NOT identify it as an anti-pattern or common AI-generated signal. It treats it as a legitimate design choice with caveats, saying 'if keeping the gradient, desaturate it slightly'. This is a missed detection.

expectations.text: Flags Inter at 400 weight on muted palette as generic/timid
expectations.evidence: Issue #2 'Inter at 400 weight on a gradient background': 'The thin strokes of a 400-weight font can visually vibrate against a busy or saturated background'. Also Issue #6 mentions 'muted gray palette alongside a saturated gradient' creates 'tonal disconnect'. The weight and palette weakness is flagged, though framed more as legibility than genericism.

expectations.text: Flags centered text spanning full 1440px as too wide for center alignment
expectations.evidence: Issue #3 'Full-width text spanning 1440px': 'Centered text that spans the entire 1440px container produces extremely long line lengths. Comfortable reading requires line lengths of 45-75 characters (roughly 600-800px).' Recommends constraining to 680-800px max-width.

expectations.text: Flags stock photo of people in office
expectations.evidence: Issue #5 'Stock photo of people in an office': 'Generic office stock photography is one of the most overused patterns on the web. It communicates nothing specific about your product and actively undermines trust.' Recommends product screenshots, custom illustrations, or no image.

expectations.text: Flags 12px body text as below 14px minimum
expectations.evidence: Issue #1 'Extreme typographic scale imbalance': '12px body text is too small for comfortable reading on the web' and '12px falls below the widely accepted floor of 16px for body text'. Accessibility table marks 'Minimum font size: Failing (practical) — 12px body text is below the practical 16px floor'.

expectations.text: Suggests specific alternative choices (different gradient, font, alignment)
expectations.evidence: Issue #4 recommends 'desaturate it slightly or darken both endpoints' or 'add a semi-transparent dark overlay (rgba(0,0,0,0.3))'. Issue #3 recommends 'Constrain the text content to a max-width of 680-800px'. Issue #1 recommends 'Increase body text to 18-20px minimum. Push the heading to 56-64px'. Specific numeric alternatives provided.

expectations.text: Identifies purple-to-pink gradient as anti-pattern
expectations.evidence: Issue 1 titled 'AI Purple Gradient — Instant Commoditization' explicitly identifies '#8B5CF6-to-#EC4899 is the default gradient of AI landing pages' as High severity, referencing the Universal Anti-Patterns rule

expectations.text: Flags Inter at 400 weight on muted palette as generic/timid
expectations.evidence: Issue 3 titled 'Inter 400 on Muted Gray — Legibility Failure' flags it as High severity, noting 'Inter at weight 400 is already a light-reading font. Pair it with muted gray text on a purple-to-pink gradient background, and you have a contrast problem'

expectations.text: Flags centered text spanning full 1440px as too wide for center alignment
expectations.evidence: Issue 4 titled 'Full-Width Text at 1440px — Unreadable Lines' states 'At 1440px container width with 18px text, you are looking at 160+ characters per line' and recommends 'Constrain the text block to max-width 680px'

expectations.text: Flags stock photo of people in office
expectations.evidence: Issue 5 titled 'Stock Office Photo — Generic and Trust-Eroding' states 'A stock photo of people in an office communicates nothing about the product. It is the visual equivalent of lorem ipsum.'

expectations.text: Flags 12px body text as below 14px minimum
expectations.evidence: Issue 2 identifies the broken type scale with '12px body text fails accessibility standards' and states 'Body text minimum 16px (Inter 400). For a hero section specifically, 18px is the floor'

expectations.text: Suggests specific alternative choices (different gradient, font, alignment)
expectations.evidence: Report provides specific alternatives: for gradient 'Pick one brand color. Build a tonal palette'; for typography 'Inter 600 at 48px heading, Inter 400 at 18px body'; for layout 'max-width 680px centered'; for image 'Product screenshot or UI mockup'

expectations.text: Identifies purple-to-pink gradient as anti-pattern
expectations.evidence: Issue 1 titled 'Purple-to-Pink Gradient Background' identifies it as High severity, calling it 'the single most overused color treatment in AI-generated and template web design' and stating '#8B5CF6-to-#EC4899 gradient (violet-to-pink) has become the default modern tech background'

expectations.text: Flags Inter at 400 weight on muted palette as generic/timid
expectations.evidence: Issue 2 titled 'Inter at 400 Weight' states 'Inter is explicitly called out in the skill guidelines as a generic, overused font to avoid' and 'Weight 400 (regular) compounds this: it is the most neutral weight of the most neutral font. Nothing about it communicates a point of view.'

expectations.text: Flags centered text spanning full 1440px as too wide for center alignment
expectations.evidence: Issue 4 titled 'Full-Width Centered Text on 1440px' states 'Body text spanning the full 1440px container means line lengths far exceed the optimal 45-75 character range' and recommends 'max-width: 680px'

expectations.text: Flags stock photo of people in office
expectations.evidence: Issue 5 titled 'Stock Photo of People in an Office' states 'People smiling in an office is the most common hero image on the internet. Users have developed banner blindness to it.'

expectations.text: Flags 12px body text as below 14px minimum
expectations.evidence: Issue 3 identifies '42px Heading / 12px Body Text — Broken Type Scale' as Critical severity, stating '12px body text is genuinely hard to read on most screens' and 'The WCAG recommendation is a minimum of 16px for body text'

expectations.text: Suggests specific alternative choices (different gradient, font, alignment)
expectations.evidence: Provides specific alternatives: for gradient 'warm amber (#F59E0B) to deep coral (#DC2626)' or 'deep navy (#0F172A)'; for fonts 'Playfair Display or Fraunces, Clash Display, Recoleta'; for alignment 'max-width: 65ch (~680px)'; for image 'Product screenshot/UI mockup'

notes: 4-way comparison: Design Forge vs Vibe Check vs Frontend Design vs No Skill
Design Forge: 95.8% pass rate, 29127 avg tokens
Vibe Check: 91.4% pass rate, 54255 avg tokens
Frontend Design: 86.2% pass rate, 23004 avg tokens
No Skill: 72.8% pass rate, 20218 avg tokens
Design Forge leads on pass rate but Vibe Check and Frontend Design produce more creative/thorough outputs
All three skills use ~2-3x more tokens than baseline but produce measurably better structure
Font count assertion (max 2) trips all skills that add monospace for code — may need revision
